resident_profile: Malaysia resident individual using common treaty/broker defaults where they are clearly source-backed. Actual rate can differ by broker, account type, instrument, treaty form, and tax status.

markets.label: United States ordinary dividends
markets.basis: US-source FDAP dividends paid to a nonresident alien are generally subject to 30% withholding. Malaysia is not on the IRS income tax treaty list, so the conservative Malaysia-resident default is 30%.
markets.caveat: W-8BEN confirms foreign status; it does not create a reduced US treaty rate for Malaysia residents.

markets.label: Malaysia listed dividends
markets.basis: Malaysia single-tier/company dividends have no source withholding to the shareholder.
markets.caveat: For Malaysian resident individuals, annual dividend income above RM100,000 is subject to 2% tax from YA2025 under Budget 2025 rules.

markets.label: Singapore ordinary dividends
markets.basis: Singapore does not normally withhold tax on ordinary company dividends paid to non-resident shareholders.
markets.caveat: S-REIT and other trust distributions can have separate rules. Use override when the broker statement shows a different rate.

markets.label: Hong Kong dividends
markets.basis: Hong Kong has no withholding tax on dividends.
markets.caveat: Mainland China source income received through Hong Kong listings can carry PRC withholding at the issuer level.

markets.label: UK ordinary dividends
markets.basis: UK ordinary dividends are generally paid without UK withholding tax.
markets.caveat: UK REIT property income distributions are different; use the UK REIT PID row for that case.

markets.label: UK REIT property income distribution
markets.label_zh: 英国 REIT 房产收入分派
markets.basis: UK REIT property income distributions are commonly paid after 20% withholding.
markets.caveat: Treaty relief, ISA/SIPP treatment, and broker setup can change the actual rate.

markets.label: Australia franked dividends
markets.basis: Fully franked Australian dividends are not subject to Australian dividend withholding tax for non-residents.
markets.caveat: Only the franked component gets this treatment. Use the unfranked row for unfranked dividends.

markets.label: Australia unfranked dividends
markets.basis: Unfranked Australian dividends can be subject to dividend withholding tax. The Malaysia-Australia tax treaty commonly caps ordinary portfolio dividend withholding at 15%.
markets.caveat: If treaty paperwork is not applied, broker withholding can differ. Use override from the broker statement.

markets.label: Canada dividends
markets.basis: Canada statutory Part XIII withholding on dividends is 25%, but tax treaties commonly reduce it. The Malaysia-Canada treaty commonly caps dividends at 15%.
markets.caveat: If treaty status is not applied by the broker, the default statutory 25% may appear. Use override when needed.

markets.label: China PRC-source dividends
markets.basis: PRC-source dividends paid to non-resident investors are commonly subject to 10% withholding.
markets.caveat: A-shares, H-shares, Stock Connect, ETFs, funds, and broker nominee chains can create different practical treatment. Use override for the actual statement rate.

fx.cost: Free public no-key endpoint. No OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity, or paid market-data API token is used.
